results.title: Nvidia Q3 FY2026 Results: Revenue $35.1 Billion, Data Center Up 112% YoY
results.snippet: Nvidia reported Q3 FY2026 revenue of $35.1 billion, up 94% year-over-year and 17% sequentially. Data Center revenue reached $30.8 billion (+112% YoY), driven by Blackwell GPU architecture ramp. Gaming revenue was $3.3 billion (+15% YoY). Gross margin was 74.6%. GAAP EPS was $0.78; non-GAAP EPS was $0.81.

results.title: Nvidia Blackwell GPU Demand Exceeds Supply; Hyperscalers Commit Multi-Year Capex
results.snippet: Nvidia's Blackwell B200 and GB200 GPUs face demand that vastly exceeds current production capacity, according to CEO Jensen Huang. Microsoft, Google, Amazon, and Meta have collectively committed over $300 billion in AI infrastructure capital expenditure for 2025-2026, with Nvidia GPUs as the primary compute platform. TSMC is ramping CoWoS advanced packaging to meet Blackwell production targets.

results.title: Nvidia CUDA Ecosystem Locks In Enterprise AI Developers; Software Moat Widens
results.snippet: Nvidia's CUDA parallel computing platform has over 5 million registered developers globally, creating a deep software moat that makes switching to rival AI accelerators costly. Nvidia's NIM microservices and AI Enterprise software stack generate recurring subscription revenue estimated at $2.5 billion annually. The company's networking business (InfiniBand, Ethernet) reached $3.1 billion in Q3 FY2026.
